text: Being here fills me with a sense of curiosity and contemplation. The first space feels sterile and utilitarian—a liminality that hums with distant purpose, perhaps a transition zone that invites reflection on movement and safety. Entering the second area, the vibrancy of color and the display of research and development artifacts sparks my mind into a different mode, one of learning and engagement. The juxtaposition between these environments makes me ponder the boundaries between the mundane and the imaginative, how human creativity can transform sterile halls into spaces of discovery. I feel both cautious and inspired.
